title: Equipment loss counters bayanat-only — removed enemy[] echo double-count
changes: Root cause: aggregate_stats.py + stats.html both counted equipment losses (tanks, hummers, bulldozers, Hermes, choppers, MRAPs) from BOTH bayanat[].fullText AND enemy[].fullText. enemy[] is a media-echo layer — a single destroyed vehicle is repeated across Al-Manar / Al-Mayadeen / IDF statement / wire-agency entries, so summing regex hits across those echoes inflated totals 3-5×.

Internal inconsistency: the code already applied echo-dedup logic to casualty numbers (bayanat-only, with an explicit comment at extract_losses about 3-5× inflation) but not to equipment. Now both equipment and casualties are bayanat-only — one authoritative mention per incident.
src/python/aggregate_stats.py — removed the `for e in data.get(enemy, [])` loss-scan loop. Module + extract_losses docstrings updated to document enemy[] exclusion rationale.
stats.html — removed the mirror-copy of that loop in the client-side aggregator so browser and Python produce identical counters.

Empirical impact over the full war window 2024-09-23 → 2024-11-27: helicopters 15 → 2 (7.5× over-count corrected), tanks 49 → 48, Hermes drones 13 → 13, bulldozers 6 → 6. Most equipment was already bayanat-dominated; helicopters were the main offender because every channel echo of an Israeli sortie read as an additional chopper-down mention.
No change to operation counts (offensive strikes, defensive ops, counter-advance, counter-landing) — those were already bayanat-only before this patch.
Validator: 0 errors, 17 warnings, 907/924 clean days — unchanged from v2.6.1 (data files untouched, only the aggregator logic changed).

title: Corpus-wide re-categorise — eliminated 231 cross-category double-counts
changes: Bulk re-ran categorize.py across all 924 raw/YYYY-MM-DD.json files. Prior to this, only the 11 Phase A days plus a handful of cherry-picked days had been re-categorised after v2.5.41 introduced PALESTINE_MARKERS / YEMEN_MARKERS / IRAQ_MARKERS. The remaining ~900 day files still carried the old-categorizer output where Palestinian/Yemeni/Iraqi posts with #إعلام_العدو were filed under `enemy`, and were then additionally picked up in `allies` on a subsequent partial merge — producing same-postTime same-fullText entries in BOTH categories.

Duplicate scan before fix: 129 enemy∩allies + 102 iran∩allies = 231 posts counted twice in stats, spread across 147 days. Scan after fix: 0 cross-category duplicates across all 924 files. Within-day siren dupes (same text + same time): 0. Cross-day exact spillover (same fullText on 2+ dates): 0 (when compared on full-text hash, not 200-char prefix).

src/python/fix_data.py — spillover restart detection now skips num=0 entries (Iraqi/unnumbered bayans) when walking backwards to find the drop point. Previously a num=0 entry between the last Palestinian bayan of the day and the next-day restart would block detection (seen on 2024-11-09, 2024-11-21).

src/python/fix_data.py — spillover late-time threshold lowered from 22:00 → 21:00. num=0 entries inside the spillover tail are exempt from the threshold (they belong wherever the numbered entries do). Caught 2024-11-09 where the restart happened at 21:19.

Net corpus delta (re-categorise + spillover fixes): bayanat 5,121 → 5,875 (+754 — v2.5.41-43 bayan improvements now applied corpus-wide), enemy 13,743 → 13,476 (−267), allies 7,959 → 7,628 (−331), iran 1,073 → 969 (−104), sirens 7,739 → 7,730 (−9), videos 1,037 → 1,028 (−9), summaries unchanged at 166.
Validator result: 0 errors, 17 warnings, 907/924 clean days (98.2%) — improved from 23 warnings / 901 clean (97.5%) at v2.6.0.

Stats shown on the website were previously inflated for enemy/allies/iran on the 147 affected days. Every daily stats header, spotlight index, monthly chronicle, and search facet has been rebuilt against the de-duplicated data.

changes: src/python/categorize.py extract_target: broaden the verb alternation with feminine/plural/passive forms (استهدفت/استهدفوا/قصفت/قصفوا/دكّت/دكّوا/هاجم/هاجمت/هاجموا/فجّرت/تصدّت/أسقطت/رصدت/اشتبكت/شنّت/نفّذت/استُهدف/قُصف), extend the place-noun list with مرفأ/ميناء/مطار/محطّة/معبر/حقل/جسر/نفق/مفرق/مدخل/بوابة/منظومة/مصفاة/مصنع/مقر/مجمّع/مستعمرة, add colon-list pattern (في: A، B، وC), add reverse-order syntax (X استهدفها المجاهدون), broaden TERM punctuation class (۔/؟/!/—/:) and weapon terminators (بالقذائف/بالصليات/بالمحلقات/ببركان/بشاهد/بكنعان/بفلق/بكروز).
src/python/categorize.py _clean_body_for_target: now strips shadda + all harakat + zero-width marks before pattern matching — no more `ّ?` noise in patterns and shadda-variants don't block matches.

src/python/categorize.py extract_weapon: rewritten with normalised-dict keys. Added named-system entries (كاتيوشا/بركان/فلق/كنعان/شاهد/كروز/بدر), حسan Hezbollah-specific phrasings (سرب/أسراب من المسيّرات / المحلّقات الانقضاضية, صليات صاروخية كبيرة, الأسلحة المناسبة, قذائف الهاون, عبوات ناسفة, تشريكة ميكانيكية). Fallback regex `ب(ال)?(صاروخ|صواريخ|قذائف|قذيفة|سلاح|أسلحة) X` for unseen named systems.

src/python/categorize.py SIREN_COORDS: +35 new locations (كرمئيل without al- prefix, كريات جات/يام/أتا/ملاخي/موتسكين/بياليك/إيلعيزر, الغجر, سهل الحولة, إييليت هشاخر, راموت نفتالي, رمات هشارون, برعام, بيت هيلل/شمش, ديشون, نؤوت مردخاي, روش بينا, هغوشريم, شآر يشوف, بني براك, كفار مساريك, دافنه, نوف هجليل/الناصرة العليا, الناصرة, هوشعيا, مسعاده/مجدل شمس/بقعاتا, يوكنعام, زرعيت, شتولا, زيكيم, أشكلون). title: Arabic normaliser + Palestinian/Yemeni/Iraqi/summaries handlers — +6,352 entries recovered
changes: src/python/categorize.py — new norm_ar() utility strips tatweel (U+0640), harakat (U+064B-0652 + U+0670), normalises alef variants (أ/إ/آ → ا) and yaa (ى → ي), converts Arabic-Indic digits (٠-٩ → 0-9), and removes zero-width/RTL marks (U+200B-U+200F / U+202A-U+202E / BOM / NBSP). Applied uniformly before every substring match and classification regex in categorize(), parse_bayan(), classify_bayan_type(), and compute_siren_points() — fixes tatweel/shadda-sensitive bayan markers, shadda'd sirens (صفّارات), Arabic-numeral bayan numbers, and case-sensitive place lookups.
categorize() dispatcher reordered — videos check moved LAST (previously swallowed بالفيديو messages that also matched iran/enemy/yemen content).

NEW: Palestinian-factions handler (حركة حماس / كتائب القسام / سرايا القدس / الجهاد الإسلامي / الجبهة الشعبية / #فلسطين_المحتلة / المقاومة الفلسطينية). Routed to allies[] with flag='فلسطين'. Previously: silently dropped into the 'other' bucket.
Yemen handler expanded: added يحيى سريع / العميد سريع / صنعاء / الحوثي / الحوثيين / الجيش اليمني / حركة أنصار الله.
Iraq handler expanded: added كتائب حزب الله العراقي / عصائب أهل الحق.
Iran handler expanded: added صاروخ إيراني / صواريخ إيرانية / طهران / قاآني / بزشكيان / القوة الجوفضائية / القوة البحرية الإيرانية.
NEW: summaries[] bucket — captures daily (الحصاد اليومي), weekly (الحصاد الأسبوعي), and cumulative (الحصاد العام) operation recaps previously silently dropped.

classify_bayan_type() priority reordered — defensive now takes priority over list_strikes so counter-advance bayanat that enumerate multiple counter-strikes stay correctly classified. parse_list_strikes() also fires on defensive bayanat with list markers, preserving per-strike detail.

SIREN_COORDS lookup in compute_siren_points() now uses norm_ar on both the dict key and the siren text, so 'المالكيّة' (with shadda) and 'المالكية' (without) both match the same coordinate. Previously relied on hand-added duplicate keys.

Schema additive change: data/*.json now includes stats.summaries + top-level summaries[]. Downstream code (validator, build_index, stats.html) tolerates the new field; it simply won't index or display summaries until wired up in a follow-up version.

Safe corpus-wide merge (no data regressions): +6,352 Palestinian/Yemeni/Iraqi entries recovered into allies[], +167 Iranian entries, +62 daily/weekly summaries. Spotlight index grew from 29,636 → 36,155 items (+22%).

All 5,079 existing bayanat preserved. 34 reclassified to correct bayan_type via the normaliser (previously mis-bucketed due to tatweel/shadda variants). Validator clean (same 2 pre-existing duplicate-num errors unchanged).

changes: src/python/aggregate_stats.py — new CLI that takes a date range and emits JSON matching the Hezbollah 'الحصاد العام' infographic schema: target-type counts (قواعد/ثكنات/مستوطنات/مواقع حدودية/طائرات/بارجة/تصدي تقدّم/تصدي إنزال), weapon-family counts (صاروخية/مسيّرات/موجّهة/نوعية/مدفعية/دفاع جوي/هندسة/بحرية/…), enemy-loss estimates, offensive/defensive/counter-advance split, daily average, max single-day.

title: Expand list-format bayanat into strikes[] sub-arrays
changes: src/python/categorize.py — new parse_list_strikes() parses bayanat that enumerate multiple strikes after 'على النحو الآتي:'. Handles 3 format variants: bullet with timestamp ('- HH:MM LOC WEAPON.'), inline with timestamp ('عند الساعة HH:MM LOC WEAPON.'), and shared-time bullets where each bullet lists only a target and inherits time + weapon from the parent sentence.

parse_bayan now attaches a strikes[] sub-array to list_strikes bayanat — each entry is {opTime, target, weapon}. Preserves 1 announcement = 1 bayan row for the UI, while giving downstream stats accurate per-istihdaf rows.
_clean_location — only strips leading 'و' when it's a standalone connective (followed by whitespace). Fixes 11 targets like 'وادي X' that were being chopped to 'ادي X'.

data/*.json — all 16 list_strikes bayanat now carry a strikes[] array totaling 81 strikes (previously counted as 16). Corpus-wide strike uplift: +65. True estimated istihdafat: 5,059 (vs 5,079 bayanat — statements removed, list_strikes expanded).
AMBIGUITY_REPORT.md — updated TL;DR with corrected numbers; the original scanner undercounted list_strikes by using a stricter bullet pattern than the actual content format.

src/python/categorize.py — strips trailing clash-verb clauses ('اشتبك معها مجاهدو…' / 'واشتبكوا معها' / 'واستهدفوا…') from captured locations; fixes 5 action-verb-in-target bugs.

src/python/categorize.py — every bayan now gets a bayan_type field: statement | offensive | defensive | list_strikes | narrative_recap. Lets downstream stats filter political statements and count defensive/counter-operations separately.

monthly/*.html — regenerated chronicle pages reflect the cleaned targets.

title: Lock spotlight filter pills to fixed width AND height
changes: src/css/spotlight.css — .sl-filter-pill now uses fixed width:112px + height:32px + flex:0 0 auto instead of min-width. Previous min-width approach still let chips widen per label and allowed vertical drift between active and inactive states. Switched vertical padding to 0 (with align-items:center handling centering) because explicit height + vertical padding fight each other and cause baseline drift. flex:0 0 auto locks each chip against the flex parent so no chip can be squished or stretched.

src/css/spotlight.css — mobile override now mirrors the same pattern: width:92px + height:30px + padding 0/12px. Every chip — الكل, بيانات, صفارات, إعلام العدو, إيران, فيديو, حلفاء — renders at pixel-identical dimensions regardless of label length or active state, on both desktop and mobile.

title: Fixed-size category filter pills in spotlight search
changes: src/css/spotlight.css — .sl-filter-pill (the category chip bar at the top of the spotlight search modal: الكل / بيانات / صفارات / إعلام العدو / إيران / فيديو / حلفاء) now has min-width:108px + justify-content:center + box-sizing:border-box, so every chip renders at the same width regardless of label length. Active state no longer changes the visual footprint since the pill is already at its min-width budget. Padding bumped from 6px/14px to 8px/16px so the chips feel comfortably hittable on desktop.

src/css/spotlight.css — mobile override (@media) bumped .sl-filter-pill to min-width:88px and padding 7px/12px so chips stay uniform on narrow viewports without overflowing the chip row. Same rationale as the earlier table pill fix: min-width (not width) lets any future longer label grow instead of clipping Arabic text silently.

title: Fixed-size category pills in search results table
changes: search.html — category pills in the search results table (.sp-tbl .pill) now render at a consistent min-width of 62px with centered text, so every badge (بيانات, صفارات, إيران, فيديوهات, غزة, اعداء, حلفاء) aligns uniformly in the category column instead of auto-sizing to label length. Chose min-width over fixed width so a future longer label would still render in full rather than being silently clipped — especially important on mobile where users can't hover to recover truncated Arabic text. Added box-sizing:border-box so the 9px horizontal padding is included in the 62px budget and pills never overflow their table cell on narrow viewports.

title: Fix invisible stats bars — display:block + flex hours layout
changes: search.html — fixed the top-weapons / top-targets bar fills rendering at 0 height (looking like empty white skeleton tracks): the .sp-st-bar-fill element was a <span> (inline by default) with height:100%, but inline elements ignore height. Added display:block to both .sp-st-bar-track and .sp-st-bar-fill so the percentage width and full track height now render correctly. Bumped track height to 20px, made it a rounded pill (border-radius:999px) with a darker surface-lo background and inset shadow so the empty track is clearly distinguishable from the panel, and gave the gold fill a soft drop shadow + min-width:6px so even tiny percentages have a visible nub.

search.html — fixed the hours histogram rendering completely empty: the panel used display:grid with align-items:end and percentage heights on grid items, which collapses in browsers when grid-template-rows isn't explicit. Switched to display:flex + align-items:flex-end with a definite parent height (130px), each column gets flex:1 for equal width and the inline percentage height now resolves correctly against the definite parent. Bars use the same gold gradient as the bar fills, with a recessed surface-lo track + inset shadow so the chart reads as a real chart instead of a placeholder skeleton.

search.html — both bar systems now share the same visual language: surface-lo recessed track, accent gradient fill (with hover gradient flip on the row bars and scale-on-hover for the hour columns), proper border + inset shadow on tracks, drop shadow on fills.

title: Map and stats polish — neutral basemap + visible hour bars + hover tooltips
changes: search.html — switched the map view's tile layer from default OpenStreetMap (which renders Israel/Lebanon labels in Hebrew) to CartoDB Positron light_nolabels. The basemap now has zero competing text in any language, leaving only our own Arabic markers and popups visible. Free, no API key, requires the © OpenStreetMap © CARTO attribution. There is no free OSM tile provider that serves Arabic-labeled tiles for this region without an API key, so the cleanest answer is no labels at all on the basemap.

search.html — fixed the hours histogram looking like a skeleton: bars now use a 12% baseline + 88% data-driven scale so even hours with 1 result get a visibly distinct bar (was previously 2px which was barely perceptible against a 110px panel). Bars use a vertical accent gradient instead of flat color, hover state scales the bar slightly with a darker accent border, and zero-result hours fade to 25% opacity for clear empty/non-empty differentiation.

search.html — added empty-state for the hours panel: when no items in the result set carry a time field, the histogram is replaced by 'لا توجد بيانات وقت في النتائج الحالية' instead of rendering 24 invisible bars.

search.html — added rich hover tooltips to the hours histogram: each column now reveals a positioned tooltip on hover (HH:00 — N نتيجة) with a small arrow pointing at the bar. CSS-only, uses ::before/::after pseudo-elements with data-tip attribute. Replaces the slow native title='' tooltip.

search.html — added hover effects to the bar rows in the weapons/targets panels: the entire row gets a subtle accent-dim background highlight on hover, the fill bar darkens to a stronger accent gradient, and padding shifts so the highlight feels deliberate.

search.html — added donut segment hover: hovering a category arc thickens its stroke from 6 to 8, and the parent SVG slightly thickens all segments together when hovered, drawing attention without losing context.

search.html — added sparkline hover with a moving cursor line + data dot + tooltip: as the user mouses across the daily activity sparkline, a vertical accent line tracks the cursor, a small dot appears on the nearest data point, and a positioned tooltip shows the exact date and count. Implemented via a single mousemove listener that converts viewport pixels to data indices, no SVG event listeners per point.

title: Seven view modes for advanced search results
changes: search.html — added a view-switcher tab bar above the results that toggles between seven distinct rendering modes for the same FILTERED array: list (default), table, timeline, grouped-by-day, heatmap calendar, geographic map, and stats dashboard. Each mode optimizes for a different cognitive task (scanning vs reading vs analysis vs geography vs aggregation). View choice is persisted in the URL as &view=X so every search-and-view combination is shareable.

search.html — refactored renderResults() into a thin dispatcher that calls one of seven renderView*() functions. getSortedFiltered() centralizes the sort logic. Switching views is instant because the underlying FILTERED array doesn't change; only the rendering layer flips.

search.html — TABLE view: dense sortable spreadsheet with date/time/category/weapon/target/source/snippet columns, alternating row backgrounds, click-row-to-deep-link, ⤓ تصدير CSV button that emits a UTF-8 BOM CSV (Arabic-correct in Excel) of the entire FILTERED set, not just the visible page.

search.html — TIMELINE view: vertical accent-colored axis with day markers, results grouped under day labels with their times in monospace, category-colored right border on each event card. Reuses the same data shape as list view but visually emphasizes chronology.

search.html — GROUPED view: collapsible day cards with per-day stat pills broken down by category (3 بيانات · 8 صفارات · 1 عدو). First 3 days are expanded by default; click any day header to toggle. Each day's results render as the existing comfortable list cards.

search.html — HEATMAP view: month-grid calendar where each day cell is colored by activity intensity using a 6-step accent scale. Side panel shows the picked day's per-category breakdown. Prev/next month buttons walk only the months that have data. HM_MONTH and HM_PICKED state survive across rerenders so picking a day doesn't lose context.

search.html — MAP view: Leaflet circle markers plotted from FACETS.coords for sirens (red, by location) and bayanat (gold, by target). Marker radius scales logarithmically with count. Auto-fits to bounds on load. Sidebar lists mapped locations sorted by count plus a footer noting how many locations had no coordinates. Leaflet CSS+JS loaded eagerly (defer) from CDN.

search.html — STATS view: 5 panels in a grid — top weapons bars, top targets bars, hour-of-day histogram (24 columns), category donut (inline SVG), full-width daily activity sparkline (inline SVG with gradient fill). All aggregations derived from FILTERED + per-item enrichment in a single O(n) walk per render.

src/python/build_search_facets.py — added 'coords' top-level section to data/search-facets.json that aggregates location → [lat, lng] pairs from sirenPoints across every daily report. Walked 919 days to extract 54 unique geocoded locations. Backs the new MAP view.

search.html — sub-filter panels and view modes share a consistent theme: surface2/surface backgrounds, accent-dim hover, accent for active state, tabular-nums on every numeric pill so digits align in monospace columns regardless of count length.

title: Fix deep-link from advanced search results
changes: search.html — result cards previously linked to report.html?date=YYYY-MM-DD#bayanat-N (URL fragment), but src/js/app.js boot logic at line 242 expects the deep-link in query-param form (?date=YYYY-MM-DD&tab=bayanat&idx=N). Clicking a result loaded the right report but never switched tabs or scrolled to the matching card.

search.html — makeCard() now generates report.html?date=X&tab=Y&idx=Z so app.js picks up the tab and idx params, switches to the right tab, and scrolls + highlights the exact card via its data-src-idx attribute (5-second .search-target highlight + 1-second fade-out). Tab IDs already matched the spotlight category names exactly (bayanat/sirens/enemy/iran/videos/allies) so no mapping was needed.

title: Clean up noisy facet vocabulary in advanced search
changes: src/python/build_search_facets.py — added a data-cleanup layer that normalizes and filters vocabulary before writing data/search-facets.json. Previously the dropdowns surfaced categorizer fallback strings (الساعة, بيان عام لحزب الله, بيان حول مجازر العدو بحق المدنيين, بيان إلى أهالي الجنوب والبقاع والضاحية) as if they were real targets, and weapon names appeared in 48 variants that were really diacritic/prefix/plural forms of ~12 canonical weapons.

src/python/build_search_facets.py — canonicalize_weapon() collapses weapon variants into a canonical form: صلية صاروخية absorbs its 6+ punctuation/prefix variants (was 1302 → now 1439 merged), مسيّرة انقضاضية absorbs 8 drone variants (was 86 → now 167), صاروخ موجه absorbs its variants (was 72 → now 163), قذائف مدفعية absorbs its variants, plus صواريخ نوعية and صاروخ أرض-جو families. Generic fallback strings (الأسلحة المناسبة, قنص, بصلية من) are dropped. Weapons above 50 chars are dropped as they're mis-classified targets.

src/python/build_search_facets.py — clean_target() drops targets from communique-badge bayanat entirely (statements have no operational target), drops the explicit الساعة fallback, drops any target starting with بيان (category label mis-stored as target), and drops targets < 3 chars. Top targets are now all real place names: الخيام, الطيبة, حيفا, كريات شمونة, شبعا اللبنانية, صفد, موقع الراهب, ثكنة زرعيت…

src/python/build_search_facets.py — clean_tag() drops بيان عام (a badge category mis-stored as a tag) from the tag vocabulary. clean_siren_location() drops the lone . entry (115 parse failures) and any location under 5 chars.

src/python/build_search_facets.py — the per-item enrichment (FACETS.bayan[date][i]) now stores CANONICALIZED values so when a user picks صلية صاروخية from the weapon dropdown the filter matches items whose original raw value was صليات صاروخية or بصلية صاروخيّة or any of the other variants. This keeps the dropdown values consistent with what the filter actually matches.

data/search-facets.json — regenerated: weapons 48 → 12, targets 1982 → 1978, tags 21 → 20, siren locations 2960 → 2957. File size slightly smaller (1086 → 1073 KB) despite storing canonical forms because the vocab lists got shorter.
index.html — footer version pill was stuck at v2.5.19 (missed in the v2.5.20 bump); corrected alongside the v2.5.21 bump.

title: Theme-aware multi-select widgets with beautiful count pills
changes: search.html — replaced the native <select> and <input list>+<datalist> sub-filter controls with a custom theme-aware multi-select widget (createMultiSelect) so the dropdowns now match the site's light/dark theme via --surface/--surface2/--accent vars instead of leaking the OS-default appearance. Each widget has a clickable button with a label + count pill, opens a panel with a search filter input, a مسح الكل action, and a scrollable checkbox list. Click outside or on another widget closes the open one.

search.html — five filters are now multi-select (was single-value): weapon, target, tag, siren region, ally flag. Selected values are stored in a Set, the button label shows the first 3 picks plus a +N counter for the rest, and the checkbox list highlights selected items with the accent color.

search.html — passBayan/passSiren/passAllies updated for Set semantics: weapons/targets use OR within the set (item matches if its weapon is any of the picked ones), badges use OR set membership, tags use OR (item matches if any of its tags is in the picked set), siren regions use OR set membership, ally flags use OR. All filter groups still AND with each other.

search.html — count pills throughout the form (.sp-ms-cnt, .sp-bchip-cnt, .sp-count, .sp-ms-count) got a beauty pass: rounded background pills, tabular-nums + lining-nums via font-feature-settings + font-variant-numeric so digits align in monospace columns, min-width centering so 1-digit and 4-digit counts share the same visual weight, accent-dim background that flips to solid accent on selection, and smooth hover transitions.
src/js/app.js — bumped the report-page version footer from v2.5.17 to v2.5.20 (the pre-push hook had been failing to land its sync fix on origin since v2.5.18; this catches it up directly).
All html footers and asset cache busters bumped 2.5.19 → 2.5.20.

title: Real dropdown vocab for advanced search sub-filters
changes: src/python/build_search_facets.py — new build script that walks every data/*.json daily report and extracts: (1) vocab lists for the search.html dropdowns — 48 weapons, top 300 targets, 5 badge categories with Arabic labels, 21 tags, top 300 siren locations, 2 ally flags — and (2) per-item enrichment keyed by date → array indexed by i, carrying the real weapon/target/badge/tags/num fields for bayanat, location for sirens, and flag for allies. Output: data/search-facets.json (~1.1 MB).

src/python/build_index.py — hooked the new facets build into the existing index pipeline as step 5 so a single `python3 src/python/build_index.py` run regenerates reports-meta, spotlight-index, nav, harbi.db, AND search-facets in one shot. The docstring was updated to list all five outputs.

search.html — replaced the four text inputs (weapon / target / siren region / ally source) with real dropdowns and datalists backed by search-facets.json. Weapon and tag use <select> (48 and 21 values with occurrence counts), target and siren location use <input list> + <datalist> for browser-native autocomplete against the top 300 values each, ally flag is a 2-option <select>.

search.html — badge chips are now dynamically rendered from search-facets.json with the real 5 categories: بيان عام (communique), ضربة عمق (deep), آلية / دبابة (tank), مستوطنة (settlement), متعدد الأهداف (multi). Each chip shows its occurrence count and toggles independently; populateFacets() creates them with their own click handler.

search.html — passBayan/passSiren/passAllies now use metaFor(item) to look up per-item enrichment from FACETS.bayan/siren/allies and do precise field-level matching: exact weapon equality, substring match on the real target/location strings, badge set membership, tag array includes check, and numeric range on the real bayan num. Falls back to substring-on-title when enrichment is missing so nothing is silently dropped.
search.html — SIREN_COUNT_BY_DAY now prefers FACETS.siren day-length (precomputed server-side) over walking the full INDEX at runtime, keeping the minPerDay filter O(1) per item.

search.html — loadIndex() fetches spotlight-index.json and search-facets.json in parallel via Promise.all so the form is hydrated with real vocab before the first render. Facets load is fault-tolerant: if the file is missing, filters fall back to substring matching and the dropdowns just show the placeholder option.

title: Advanced search page with conditional sub-filters
changes: search.html — new standalone advanced search page that loads data/spotlight-index.json once and runs client-side filtering across all 923 days of the archive. Free-text input supports "exact phrase", -exclude and bare-word AND semantics with lenient Arabic normalization (ا/أ/إ/آ, ى/ي, ة/ه all unified).

search.html — search scope selector (كل الحقول / العناوين فقط / النص المُبسَّط) lets the user target which indexed fields the free-text query runs against. Date-range section has six quick presets (اليوم → كل الأرشيف) plus manual from/to pickers.

search.html — content-type chips (بيانات/صفارات/عدو/إيران/فيديوهات/حلفاء) are multi-select and reveal conditional sub-filter panels: bayan panel has weapon/target/badge chips/num-range/tags, siren panel has region text + minPerDay threshold, allies panel has a source text filter. syncSubPanels() toggles .open on the relevant panels based on active chips.

search.html — runSearch() applies all filters in AND semantics against the pre-built spotlight index. bayanNum() parses the number out of the subtitle field (بيان #N) for precise numeric range filtering, SIREN_COUNT_BY_DAY is a lazily-memoized per-day count for the minPerDay filter, and passBayan/passSiren/passAllies gate items by their type-specific sub-filters.

search.html — results render via DOM API (document.createElement + replaceChildren) instead of innerHTML templates so user query text flowing through highlightInto() can never inject HTML. Match highlighting wraps terms in <mark> via text nodes only. Each result card links to report.html?date=YYYY-MM-DD#cat-i.

search.html — fully responsive with breakpoints at 780px (form collapses to single column, scope chips switch to a 3-column grid, action bar stacks column-reverse, result cards condense) and 420px (smaller header and form padding). English digits enforced everywhere via font-feature-settings lnum/tnum and toLocaleString('en-US').

search.html — URL state sync: filter values are encoded as ?q=&from=&to=&t=bayanat,sirens&scope=title&tf=&tt= so every search is shareable as a URL. restoreFromUrl() rehydrates form state on page load and نسخ رابط البحث copies the current URL to clipboard with visual feedback.

src/js/ui/navbar.js — added بحث متقدم link to the main nav between الأرشيف and التسلسل الزمني, and added path.indexOf('search') page detection so the navbar highlights the active tab when on search.html.

mockups/advanced-search.html — reference mockup with the full filter vocabulary (including subfilters that require a richer index than spotlight-index carries today) kept in the mockups folder as the visual north star for the next index-enrichment pass.

title: List view filters by the selected month
changes: archive.html — list view now filters its rows by the month shown in the toolbar header, so clicking الشهر السابق / الشهر التالي while in list view re-renders the list scoped to the newly selected month key (YYYY-MM). renderList() filters reportStats dates against availableMonths[currentIdx] unless the user has opted in to «عرض الكل».

archive.html — added a listShowAll state flag and a «عرض الكل» button at the start of the sort bar. Clicking it toggles between month-scoped and all-months views; the button label swaps to «هذا الشهر فقط» and gains an .active gold style while the unfiltered view is active.

archive.html — changeMonth() now checks if list view is active and, if so, resets listShowAll to false, updates the scope button label, and re-renders the list so the header month and visible rows stay in sync.

archive.html — list sort bar markup wraps the existing ترتيب حسب / select / direction button in a .sort-group wrapper so flex space-between can pin the scope button to one edge and the sort controls to the other without all five children being distributed across the row.

src/css/index.css — .list-sort-bar switched from justify-content flex-end to space-between and added a .sort-group inline flex helper. New .scope-btn styles (gold hover + gold .active pill) and .sort-group layout round out the control row.

title: Identical header in archive calendar and list views
changes: archive.html — removed the .list-summary div (تقرير / مجموع الأحداث / معدّل يوم) from the top of the list section so the list view no longer has an extra stats row above the sort bar.

archive.html — deleted the summary-building block inside renderList() that populated #listSummary with three stat items (rows.length, totalEvents, avg); renderList now goes straight from sorting to building the list grid.
archive.html — setView() no longer toggles a .list-mode class on the archive toolbar, so the month navigation and الوثيقة الشهرية chip stay visible in list view exactly like in calendar view.
src/css/index.css — removed the .archive-toolbar.list-mode rules (desktop and mobile) that used to hide the month nav / doc chip and re-center the view toggle when list view was active.
src/css/index.css — dropped the unused .list-summary styles (desktop flex row and mobile sizing) since the element no longer exists.

title: Archive toolbar spans full viewport width
changes: src/css/index.css — removed the 800px max-width cap on .archive-toolbar and restored the edge-to-edge banner look (border-bottom, no side border, no radius, no horizontal margin). The toolbar now spans the full viewport width while the calendar grid below continues to sit inside its 800px .container.

title: Align archive toolbar width with calendar grid
changes: src/css/index.css — constrained .archive-toolbar to max-width 800px (matching the .container that holds the calendar grid below) so the toolbar no longer stretches edge-to-edge while the calendar sits centered at 800px.

src/css/index.css — promoted the toolbar from a full-width strip with border-bottom to a self-contained rounded card (border + 14px radius + 16px top / 20px bottom margin) so it visually reads as a single grouped control that sits above the calendar card.

title: Custom zoom icon for analytics toolbox
changes: src/js/analytics/charts.js — overrode the ECharts dataZoom default crop-corners icon with a custom magnifying-glass + plus SVG path on the toolbox feature, so the تكبير control now reads as a zoom lens instead of a selection frame. The back arrow is left untouched to preserve ECharts' default.

mockups/zoom-icon-preview.html — new design-reference page showing the old icon next to three candidate replacements (magnifier with plus, bracket frame, plain magnifier) in the toolbar's gold accent, used to pick the final design.

title: Archive toolbar rework — single-row header
changes: archive.html — merged the three stacked rows (view toggle, stats strip, month navigation) into a single unified .archive-toolbar that contains the view toggle, prev/title/next month navigation, and a new monthly-document chip.
archive.html — removed the stats strip (تقرير إجمالي / هذا الشهر / شهر مغطى) along with the renderStats() function and its call inside renderCalendar().

archive.html — setView() now toggles a .list-mode class on the toolbar so the month navigation and monthly-doc chip hide automatically when the list view is active, while the view toggle re-centers itself.

src/css/index.css — replaced the legacy .stats-strip and standalone .month-nav styles with the new .archive-toolbar flex row, redesigned .view-toggle as a segmented pill inside the toolbar, restyled .month-nav with a compact title-block, and added the gold .doc-chip with gradient background, icon badge, hover shimmer, and arrow slide.

src/css/index.css — new mobile media query for .archive-toolbar collapses the row into a CSS grid with two areas (toggle+chip on top, month navigation below a dashed divider) so the layout stays one cohesive card on phones.
mockups/archive-toolbar-templates.html — refined V1 mockup with the new doc chip and a 375px mobile phone preview for design reference.

changes:
index.html — removed the entire

جاهز؟

heading, the explanatory paragraph ("أنت الآن تعرف كلّ ما تحتاجه. باقي الأرشيف سيُعلّمك نفسه عن طريق الاستخدام."), and the inline duplicate version pill from the .final-cta block at the bottom of the landing walkthrough. title: Add favicon — gold H monogram on dark, fix silent 404
changes: favicon.svg — created the asset that every page (report, analytics, archive, timeline, monthly chronicles) had been linking to but which never existed in the repo. Every page load was firing a silent 404 for /favicon.svg; the browser tab was rendering the default globe glyph instead of the brand mark. The new file is a 32x32 viewBox SVG built from pure <rect> elements (zero font dependency, so Chrome's favicon rasterizer can't fall back to a wrong glyph): a #0e0e10 rounded square base, a faint #c9a84c stroked inner border at 55% opacity, and a three-rect 'H' monogram (two 3.2px verticals + one 15px horizontal crossbar) in the site's --accent gold. Picks colors directly from existing CSS variables so it stays coherent with the report header glow without introducing a new palette entry.

index.html — added the missing <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="favicon.svg"> in <head>. Every other top-level HTML file already had this tag; index.html was the lone holdout, which meant the homepage had no favicon at all (not even a broken one). Inserted right after the <title> tag for parity with report.html / analytics.html / archive.html / timeline.html.

index.html — also fixed two stale 'Harbi Reports v2.5.5' version-pill strings (lines 780, 796) that had been missed by the v2.5.6 and v2.5.7 bump commits. Both now read v2.5.8 like the rest of the site.
Bumped cache-bust ?v= and version-pill strings to 2.5.8 across index, report, analytics, archive, timeline HTML files and the app.js footer pill.

title: Tighten header — drop hairline and vertical whitespace above cockpit row
changes: src/css/report.css — removed the gold border-top hairline from .header .date-row. It was a leftover from the old two-row layout where the hairline visually separated the title block from the date meta line; with the title block gone and the nav chips inline, the hairline was just dead chrome.

src/css/report.css — collapsed .header padding from 36px 20px 20px down to 14px 20px, and dropped the 14px top margin + 12px top padding on .date-row. The cockpit row now sits directly inside the header's radial-glow gradient with no extra vertical gutters, saving ~34px of vertical space above the date meta.
Bumped cache-bust and version-pill strings to 2.5.7 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, report HTML files and the app.js footer pill.

title: Unified header row — cockpit layout merges date meta with day nav
changes: src/css/report.css — rewrote .header .date-row as a 3-column CSS grid (auto 1fr auto). The previous two-row layout (.date-row above, .day-nav below) is now one row: next-day chip on the right, centered .date-center meta stack, previous-day chip on the left. Removed the standalone .day-nav ruleset (no longer rendered) and replaced with .header .date-row .day-nav-btn scoped selectors that keep unicode-bidi: isolate for bidi-safe Arabic + arrow glyphs. Added @media(max-width:600px) override that collapses the grid to a two-row fallback on narrow screens, pushing .date-center to order:-1 so the meta line stays on top and the nav chips wrap under it.

src/css/report.css — restructured the meta stack into .meta-line1 (day name + Arabic date, larger) and .meta-line2 (hijri · dd/mm/yyyy picker · report counter, smaller/dimmer). The day-nav-current counter is now part of meta-line2 instead of a standalone span, which eliminates the old two-row visual gap between the header and the nav bar.

src/js/app.js — renderReport() no longer builds a flat date-center; it constructs .meta-line1 + .meta-line2 and leaves an empty <span class='day-nav-current'> placeholder for initNav() to fill. This keeps nav.js fully decoupled from the specific DOM of line2.

src/js/ui/nav.js — initNav() now targets .header .date-row instead of creating a sibling .day-nav container. The next-day chip is inserted as the first grid child (right flank in RTL), the previous-day chip is appended as the last grid child (left flank in RTL), and the counter span is populated in place. No new DOM container is created, which means the layout shift between 'header rendered' and 'nav injected' is now limited to the two chips appearing on the outer columns of the existing grid.

src/python/build_index.py — synchronized the embedded initNav() template that regenerates src/js/ui/nav.js on every index rebuild, so future build_index runs don't regenerate the old two-row behavior.

mockups/header-nav-unified.html — kept as a design reference alongside controls-templates.html. Template 05 (Cockpit Arrows) is the one adopted in production; templates 01–04 and 06–07 remain for future header redesigns.
Bumped cache-bust and version-pill strings to 2.5.6 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, and report HTML files.

title: Analytics controls v2 — collapsed filter drawer + segmented grouping
changes: analytics.html — rebuilt the .a-controls row. The six category buttons (بيانات، صفارات، إعلام العدو، إيران، فيديو، حلفاء) are now collapsed behind a single 'فلاتر' drawer button with a live 6/6 badge, and the three grouping buttons (يومي، أسبوعي، شهري) became a true segmented control with a sliding golden thumb. The .a-controls class was kept as-is so mountExcelButton('.a-controls') still attaches the Excel action to the far end of the row without any change to excel-button.js.

src/js/analytics/controls.js — replaced the old per-button listeners with initDrawer() and initSegmentedGroup(). The state contract is identical: _state.categories is still a mutating array of the same six keys and _state.grouping is still 'daily'/'weekly'/'monthly', so buildWhere() and getGroupFormat() keep working unchanged. Drawer supports outside-click and Escape to close, tracks a 'partial' state when 1 ≤ checked < 6 (button flips to gold border), and the segmented thumb is positioned in RTL via getBoundingClientRect() right-offset math so it slides correctly under direction:rtl without CSS translate fiddling.

src/css/analytics.css — appended the .a-controls-v2, .a-drawer-*, and .a-seg-* component styles. All colors flow through existing CSS variables (--surface2, --accent, --border, --text-dim), so the light theme automatically inherits correct contrast; explicit body.analytics.light overrides added only for the drawer panel background and segmented-button active text color where the variable palette needs inversion. Old .a-ctrl / .a-ctrl-sep rules are left in place for backward compatibility but are no longer referenced by analytics.html.

mockups/controls-templates.html — added an interactive side-by-side preview of all five control-row design templates that drove the decision to adopt the drawer pattern. Kept in the repo as a design reference and as a test bed for future control-row iterations.
Bumped cache-bust and version pill strings to 2.5.5 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, report, and src/js/app.js.

title: Remove site-wide PDF export, keep analytics Excel export
changes: Deleted src/js/export/pdf-button.js and every call site that mounted it. The print-to-PDF button was cluttering report.html, timeline.html, and all 31 monthly chronicle pages with a duplicate of what the browser's native Ctrl+P already does.

Stripped the mountPdfButton() <script> block from report.html, timeline.html, and all monthly/*.html files. Also removed the same block from the build_monthly.py template so future chronicle rebuilds stay clean.

Kept the analytics Excel export (src/js/export/excel-button.js + build-workbook.js) untouched — that one produces a real 6-sheet .xlsx artifact with KPIs, events over time, hourly density, top targets, and raw data, which the browser cannot replicate.
Left src/css/print.css intact: it is still linked as media="print" on every page, so manual browser printing still gets proper styling. It is not an export button, just a print stylesheet.
Bumped version-pill strings to 2.5.4 across index, timeline, analytics, archive, report, and app.js.

title: Fix bidi-mangled day navigation bar
changes: src/css/report.css — added a real .day-nav flex container with gap, border, and proper styling. Before: the day-nav div had NO CSS rule at all, and the .header .day-nav-btn rules never matched because day-nav is injected as a sibling of .header (not a child), so the three nav elements collapsed into a single unstyled inline run.

Bidi fix: in RTL context, the unstyled inline flow let the LTR counter ('917 / 918') visually fuse with the Arabic date text in the adjacent button, producing garbage like '→ 11 نيسان917 / 9189 نيسان ←'. Added unicode-bidi: isolate on every .day-nav-btn and reached the .day-nav-current span (previously only scoped under .date-center) so numbers render as their own isolated run.
Hover and .disabled selectors extended to match buttons inside .day-nav, not just under .header — disabled end-of-range buttons now correctly fade to 0.3 opacity, and hover state actually triggers.

title: Simpler final CTA — button only
changes: index.html — removed the 'جاهز؟' heading and the 'أنت الآن تعرف كلّ ما تحتاجه...' lead paragraph from the .final-cta block at the bottom of the landing page. The primary 'ابدأ التصفّح' button and the version pill stay exactly where they were.

Motivation: by the time a reader reaches the final CTA they have already seen the walkthrough — the heading + copy were restating what the whole page above already teaches, so dropping them makes the call-to-action more direct and reduces visual noise on the closing screen.
No CSS changes: .final-cta already centers its children, so the button sits naturally below the FAQ without touching layout styles.

title: Hover tooltip on hours-distribution graphs
changes: src/js/dashboards/helpers.js — buildTimeline now wires every .cat-tbar to a lazy-created shared .cat-tbar-tooltip element via mouseenter/mouseleave + focus/blur handlers. Replaces the slow native bar.title attribute that took ~1s to appear and had no styling.

Tooltip content: HH:00 → HH:00 in LTR monospace plus an Arabic count line that pluralizes (حدث / أحداث). Built with DOM nodes — no innerHTML — so it stays XSS-safe even if data ever gets less trusted.
Smart positioning: tooltip is anchored above the bar, but flips below when there is no room above, and clamps horizontally to the viewport so edge bars never push it offscreen.
Bars now have tabIndex=0 and a focus-visible ring, so keyboard users get the same hover treatment as mouse users — fixes a small accessibility gap on every dashboard.
src/css/enhancements.css — added .cat-tbar-tooltip styles with dark + light theme variants, fade-in animation, RTL count row + LTR monospace time row.
All four hour graphs (bayanat, sirens, enemy, iran dashboards) inherit the new tooltip from a single helper change — no per-dashboard edits needed.

title: Finance-grade interactive line chart on the analytics dashboard
changes: Replaced the hand-rolled SVG line-chart renderer (~200 LOC) with Apache ECharts 5.5.1, served from jsDelivr and only loaded on analytics.html. The renderer keeps its old signature renderLineChart(id, datasets, height, {xLabels, grouping}), so dashboard.js and every refresh path (date range, category toggle, daily/weekly/monthly) work unchanged.

Stock-terminal interactions now available on the 'الأحداث بمرور الوقت' panel: mouse wheel zooms in/out on the time axis, the bottom range-slider pans and sets the visible window, the toolbox magnifier rect-zooms by drag, the brush tool highlights an X-range, the restore button resets, and saveAsImage exports a 2x-DPI PNG named harbi-analytics.png.

Crosshair tooltip with all series values at the cursor position, RTL formatting, theme-aware colors read from CSS vars (--accent, --border, --text-dim, --surface) so future light-theme work just inherits.

Layout: group badge (يومي/أسبوعي/شهري) anchored top-left, toolbox icons top-right, legend on its own centered row below the toolbox so the two never collide. Container height bumped to fit the slider and two header rows; ResizeObserver keeps the canvas in sync with fullscreen toggling.
Bumped all version-pill strings to 2.5.0 across the 6 user-facing HTML pages and app.js. Cache-bust query params auto-update via the existing string replace.

title: Remove multi-column sort from analytics data table
changes: Reverted the analytics 'Most Active Days' table to single-column sorting per user request — multi-sort, the sort-chain chip bar, and the priority badges added in v2.3.7/v2.3.8 are all gone

src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — restored single _tableSortKey + _tableSortDir state, removed buildOrderBy() and the multi-sort onSort handler, removed onSortRemove. ORDER BY is now built inline as 'key dir, date DESC' (date stays as a stable tiebreaker for pagination determinism)
src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderTable now takes opts.sortKey/opts.sortDir only, no opts.sort array, no chip bar DOM, no sortIndex/showPriority logic. Header click no longer forwards shiftKey
src/css/analytics.css — already had the .a-sort-bar/.a-sort-chip*/.a-sort-badge/th.sorted blocks removed in an earlier edit; verified no remnants
Kept the column-key whitelist in dashboard.js (_sortableKeys) — it's still useful as a defense-in-depth guard around the SQL ORDER BY string concat, even with single-sort
Kept the cache-bust ?v= query strings on every analytics asset import from v2.3.9 — those are still useful for forcing browsers to refetch on every release

title: Cache-bust analytics module imports so multi-sort actually loads
changes: Root cause diagnosis: analytics multi-sort was reported as 'still not working' even after v2.3.7 and v2.3.8. A fresh headless-browser test on the same commit confirmed the SQL sort WAS working (4 successive click tests produced 4 distinct row orders) — the user's browser was running a stale cached copy of dashboard.js / charts.js from before the multi-sort refactor.

analytics.html — added ?v=2.3.8 cache-bust query params to every local JS and CSS import (dashboard.js, spotlight.js, enhancements.css, analytics.css, navbar.css, spotlight.css, print.css). ES modules in Chrome are aggressively cached and stay stale until the URL changes.

Since my Python bumper already auto-replaces '?v=<old>' → '?v=<new>' on every version bump, wiring the query params with the current version now means all future analytics asset changes will cache-bust automatically on each release, no more manual intervention needed.
Bumped all version-pill strings to 2.3.9 across the 6 user-facing HTML pages and app.js.

title: Visible sort-chain status bar for analytics table
changes: Fixed perceived 'sort doesn't work' issue: the underlying SQL multi-sort was correct (verified in a headless browser — rows tying on the primary key were correctly ordered by the secondary key), but the UX was invisible when primary-key values were unique. The secondary sort had no visible effect, so users thought nothing happened.

Added a sort-chain status bar above the data table — renders one accent-colored chip per active sort column, each showing its priority rank, the column label, a direction toggle button (▲/▼), and a × remove button. This makes the multi-sort state always visible even when primary-unique values mask the tiebreaker.
Chip direction button reuses the existing onSort(key, shift=true) path to preserve the column's position in the chain while flipping its direction.
Chip × button routes through a new onSortRemove(key) callback in dashboard.js, which filters the key out of _tableSort and falls back to the default [{total, desc}] sort if the chain becomes empty.
Bigger, more prominent header priority badges (22px circle, 0.85rem bold) — previously 18px/0.7rem and barely visible; direction arrow on sorted headers bumped from 1rem to 1.2rem 900-weight.
Added a subtle highlight on sorted column headers: .sorted gets --accent-dim background; .sorted-primary also gets an inset 3px --accent underline so the primary sort is unambiguous at a glance.
Added an Arabic hint ('Shift+نقرة لفرز متعدد') at the left edge of the sort bar so first-time users discover the multi-sort interaction.
Fullscreen scales: sort bar, chips, and priority badges all bump up (1.1rem fonts, 26px chip widgets) so they stay legible when the panel fills the viewport.

title: Multi-column sort on the analytics data table
changes: Analytics 'Most Active Days' table now supports multi-column sorting — click a header to replace the current sort, Shift+click to add it as a secondary/tertiary sort or toggle its direction if already in the list
Each active sort column shows its arrow and, when more than one column is sorted, a numeric priority badge (1/2/3...) so it's clear which column drives the primary order
ORDER BY is now built from the full sort chain with an implicit 'date DESC' tiebreaker so pagination stays stable even when every active sort key ties

src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — replaced single _tableSortKey/_tableSortDir state with a _tableSort array of {key,dir}. Added a _sortableKeys whitelist to guard the SQL ORDER BY string concat from any stray key names

src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderTable now accepts opts.sort (multi-sort array), still honors the legacy opts.sortKey/opts.sortDir for backward compatibility, and forwards ev.shiftKey to the onSort callback
Added an Arabic title tooltip on the table explaining the single-click vs Shift+click interaction model

title: Larger analytics data table — second pass
changes: Further bumped .a-table font-size from 0.92rem → 1.2rem normal and 1.1rem → 1.5rem in fullscreen; header 0.78rem → 1rem normal and 0.92rem → 1.2rem in fullscreen
Widened cell padding to match the larger type: 16px/18px normal, 20px/24px in fullscreen so rows don't feel cramped at the new scale
Sort-arrow indicator bumped 0.8rem → 1rem with 6px margin for visual balance against the bigger header labels
Pager buttons bumped to 1.05rem with 10px/22px padding and 8px radius; pager info also 1.05rem to stay consistent with the new table scale

title: Bigger, more readable analytics data table
changes: src/css/analytics.css — .a-table font-size bumped from 0.64rem → 0.92rem body / 0.78rem header, with wider 12px/14px cell padding; fullscreen mode goes further to 1.1rem body / 0.92rem header with 16px/18px padding
src/js/analytics/charts.js — removed the inline font-size:0.6rem override on date cells that was defeating the CSS rule; date cells now inherit the table font-size and keep their monospace styling
Bumped the inline sort-arrow indicator from 0.5rem → 0.8rem with a small margin so it reads correctly next to the new larger header labels
src/css/analytics.css — pager buttons and info text bumped from ~0.6rem → 0.85rem with roomier 8px/18px padding so pagination controls stay visually consistent with the bigger table

title: Bigger, more visible line-chart axis labels
changes: src/js/analytics/charts.js — line-chart Y-axis labels bumped from 8px → 14px and X-axis labels from 7.5px → 13px, with color moved from --text-dim to --text and font-weight 600 so they actually read clearly in both normal and fullscreen mode
Widened axis margins: marginL 40→56 and marginB 24→38 so the bigger labels have breathing room and don't collide with the plot area or get clipped
Tightened label offsets (Y: marginL-4→marginL-8 and +3→+5 vertical nudge; X: h-4→h-10) so the larger glyphs sit where the eye expects them

Removed preserveAspectRatio='none' from the SVG — it was causing the new larger glyphs to stretch horizontally in fullscreen since the container aspect ratio differs from the viewBox. The viewBox is already sized to match the container so uniform scaling is the correct default

Replaced the pixel-threshold fullscreen heuristic in renderLineChart with a deterministic closest('.a-panel-fs') check — prevents a re-draw feedback loop where legend toggles could otherwise keep nudging the measured height above a static threshold

title: Analytics panel fullscreen actually fills the viewport
changes: Fixed: entering fullscreen on an analytics panel left the chart at its normal ~220px height with the rest of the screen empty — the line chart's SVG was being rendered at a caller-fixed pixel height, so growing the container did nothing
src/css/analytics.css — .a-panel-fs is now a flex column; .a-line-chart/#heatmapPanel/#barChart/#dataTable get flex:1 1 auto + min-height:0 so they claim all remaining viewport height after the title

src/js/analytics/charts.js — renderLineChart now measures el.clientHeight and, when the container is taller than the caller's base height, re-derives the SVG dimensions to fill it (preserveAspectRatio=none keeps the axes crisp end-to-end)
src/js/analytics/dashboard.js — entering/exiting fullscreen on a chart panel now triggers refresh() inside a requestAnimationFrame so charts re-measure and re-render at the new container size
Bonus: fullscreen bar chart now shows taller bars, larger labels, and 10px gap between rows so it actually uses the space too; heatmap gets a wider gap + larger hour labels

title: Fix site-wide light theme consistency (index, spotlight, analytics)
changes: Fixed the broken inline <head> theme-init script on every page: it called document.body.classList.add('light') while document.body was still null, throwing a silent TypeError so the saved 'light' class never applied on first paint

Replaced the one-liner with a robust IIFE that sets the class on <html> synchronously (pre-FOUC) then on <body> via a DOMContentLoaded fallback — patched across 35 HTML files (index, archive, report, timeline, and all 32 monthly/*.html pages)
Added the missing inline theme script to analytics.html, which previously had none and always rendered dark regardless of user preference

Made initNavbar() in src/js/ui/navbar.js authoritative: it now re-applies the saved theme to <html> and <body> on every page load, guaranteeing a single source of truth even if an inline script ever regresses
index.html inline <style> now defines a body.light { ... } palette block matching the site-wide light tokens — the landing page previously had no light overrides at all, so it was stuck dark
src/css/index.css now ships a body.light override block, which also fixes every monthly/*.html chronicle page (they share this stylesheet)
src/css/spotlight.css now has a body.light fork for all --sl-* variables plus softer overlay dim and modal shadow — the Spotlight search overlay was hard-coded dark and ignored the active theme
Also hardened src/js/ui/theme.js initTheme() the same null-body-safe way to stay consistent with initNavbar
Bumped footer version pills site-wide to v2.3.2, including two stale v2.2.14 pills in index.html that were missed by the 2.3.1 sync

title: Site-wide footer trailer + version pill sync
changes: Added the source-attribution / non-official disclaimer / version-pill footer to the tutorial landing page (index.html), which previously shipped without it — now every user-facing page carries the same trailer
Scoped the new landing-page footer as .site-footer (inlined CSS) since index.html does not link src/css/index.css — avoids any collision with the shared .footer rule used elsewhere
Ported the full showChangelog() overlay + data-disclaimer-slot markup into index.html so the version pill opens the changelog modal consistently across the site
Synced stale version pills: archive.html (v2.2.13), index.html final-cta CTA pill (v2.2.14), and the runtime-rendered footer in src/js/app.js used by report.html (v2.2.14) now all read v2.3.1
Unified the disclaimer.js?v= cache-bust query param on archive.html (v2.2.11 → v2.3.1) so it no longer lags behind every other page

title: Export reports as PDF (editorial pages) + Excel (analytics)
changes: Added download button on report.html, timeline.html, and all monthly chronicle pages — one click triggers a native PDF via window.print() with a dedicated @media print stylesheet
New src/css/print.css: hides navbar/spotlight/buttons, preserves RTL Arabic, forces cards to stay whole across pages, and makes monthly chronicles break one day per page for archival use

Added Excel export on analytics.html — lazy-loads SheetJS (~400KB) only on first click, then builds a 6-sheet workbook (KPIs, Events Over Time, Hourly Density, Most Active Days, Top Targets, Raw Data) by running the same queries the dashboard uses
New src/js/export/: pdf-button.js mounts the shared button, build-workbook.js is a pure testable function (14 browser assertions), excel-button.js wires the analytics click-to-download flow
Zero new runtime dependencies on the critical path — SheetJS loads only when someone actually clicks export

Added site favicon (favicon.svg at project root): dark navy rounded square with gold crosshair target ring and Arabic letter ح centered, matching the existing #080c12/#c9a84c dossier palette; wired into index.html, report.html, archive.html, analytics.html, timeline.html, test-db.html, and the monthly template in src/python/build_monthly.py

title: Tutorial-style landing page + calendar moved to Archive button

Calendar app (the previous index.html) moved to archive.html (git mv preserves history)

title: Maps now render real city borders with info drawer + POLY/DOT toggle

Pre-baked 134 place borders via new src/python/fetch_borders.py (47 real OSM polygons from Nominatim, 87 synthetic fallbacks) into data/borders.json
Added slide-in info drawer per map (4th map button ≡) with intensity legend + click-to-zoom location list, following Option B from the panel-layouts prototype
Added POLY/DOT mode toggle (5th map button) so viewers can flip between border and legacy dot rendering on demand
Extracted all polygon/dot/synthesis/drawer logic into shared src/js/maps/border-renderer.js so future maps inherit the same UX
Color ramp by intensity: green 1, yellow 2–3, orange 4–6, red 7+

title: Satellite view toggle on siren and bayanat maps

Uses ESRI World Imagery satellite tiles (zoom up to 18) vs Carto dark/light street tiles
Theme swap (light/dark) now respects satellite mode and keeps imagery when toggled
Wired into siren-map, siren-auto-map and bayanat-map via shared satellite-toggle module

analytics.html and timeline.html footers aligned with index layout
